stories.headline: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’
stories.summary: Uganda has received its first deportation flight from the US under a third-country agreement. A dozen people arrived at Entebbe International Airport, marking the first known transfer under the bilateral arrangement. The Uganda Law Society described the process as "undignified" and "dehumanizing" and plans to challenge the deportations in court. The agreement, concluded on August 21, 2025, allows Uganda to accept certain deported migrants, including non-Ugandan nationals, with conditions such as no criminal records and exclusion of unaccompanied minors. The deportees were brought in on a private charter, and the law society criticized the process as "harrowing" and vowed to take action. Operational rules and timelines for the agreement are still under negotiation, and legal challenges are expected to follow. The transfer reflects a new arrangement between Uganda and the US, allowing for the deportation of migrants to third countries.

stories.headline: Iran says strikes hit historic Pasteur Institute in Tehran: What to know Iranian officials say US-Israeli strikes have hit Tehran’s Pasteur Institute, a century-old pillar of Iran’s public health system.
stories.summary: Iranian officials report that the Pasteur Institute of Iran in Tehran has been hit by US-Israeli strikes. The century-old medical research center, established in 1920, has suffered severe damage. The Iranian Health Ministry confirmed the attack, which has been described as "heartbreaking" and "outrageous". The institute is a key pillar of Iran's public health system and has played a significant role in combating infectious diseases. Visuals released by Iranian state media show extensive damage to the facility. The Iranian government has condemned the attack, which is part of ongoing US-Israeli strikes on Tehran. The strike has destroyed the institute, according to the Iranian government, with the Health Ministry stating that it was severely damaged by a US-Israeli airstrike.

stories.headline: America Lost. Iran Won. Trump Shat the Bed. America will leave Iran weaker; the Islamic Republic will be stronger.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump delivered a prime-time speech on the Iran conflict, announcing "major combat operations" against Iran with joint US-Israeli strikes. Trump stated that regime change in Iran is not a goal for Washington and claimed the US campaign in Iran could be wrapped up within two to three weeks. A Pew Research survey found 61% of Americans disapprove of Trump's handling of the situation. Trump's remarks come after a history of making false and misleading claims, with the Washington Post counting over 30,000 during his first term. The US aims to leave Iran weaker, but some argue the Islamic Republic will emerge stronger. The outcome of the conflict remains uncertain, with differing opinions on the US's success in Iran. Trump's speech outlined the US's goals and timeline for the campaign.

stories.headline: Global waste generation could rise 50% by 2050: World Bank Inequities in waste collection and funding are a major global issue, according to an updated report. Upfront investments today will mitigate future expenses tha
stories.summary: The World Bank reports that global waste generation could increase by 50% by 2050, reaching 3.9 billion tonnes annually. In 2022, the world generated 2.6 billion tonnes of municipal solid waste. The World Bank's updated report, What a Waste 3.0, highlights inequities in waste collection and funding as a major global issue. The report emphasizes that upfront investments in waste management can mitigate future expenses related to climate change and global health. The World Bank Group has provided $5.1 billion in financing for solid waste management between 2003 and 2021, offering data, technical expertise, and private-capital solutions to support countries and cities in improving waste management practices. Without significant policy and investment shifts, waste volumes are projected to soar, exacerbating environmental and health concerns.

stories.headline: The Last of Us Online was around '80%' complete upon cancellation
stories.summary: The Last of Us Online, a multiplayer spinoff, was approximately 80% complete when it was canceled by Naughty Dog. Vinit Agarwal, the former director of the project, revealed this information, stating that the game was nearly finished after seven years of development. Agarwal expressed that he was devastated by the cancellation, which he only found out about 24 hours before Sony made the announcement public. The game had been in development for a significant amount of time, and its cancellation came as a surprise to the team. Agarwal's comments provide insight into the extent of the game's completion and the impact of its cancellation on the development team. The cancellation of The Last of Us Online has sparked interest and discussion among fans and gamers, who were looking forward to the multiplayer experience.

stories.headline: Spyware firm used fake WhatsApp app in targeted surveillance operation
stories.summary: Meta, the owner of WhatsApp, identified a targeted surveillance operation using a fake version of the WhatsApp app. Around 200 users, primarily in Italy, were tricked into installing the counterfeit app, which functioned as spyware. The attackers relied on deception, distributing the malicious version through fake apps, rather than hacking the legitimate WhatsApp app. This incident is linked to a pattern of fake app-based surveillance operations tied to Italian authorities. The spyware, dubbed Spyrtacus, has been used in similar campaigns, including fake customer support tools for cellphone providers. Meta alerted the affected users, highlighting the expanding surveillance crisis in Europe. The incident involves Italian-made spyware, and users are warned to be cautious of fake apps to avoid falling victim to such surveillance operations.

stories.headline: Nexon agrees to publish Overwatch in Korea
stories.summary: Nexon Korea Corp. has secured the rights to publish Blizzard Entertainment's Overwatch in South Korea. The company will assume publishing responsibilities from Blizzard later this year, with terms of the agreement not disclosed. Originally released in 2016, Overwatch has over 100 million lifetime players worldwide. Nexon will publish the game on PC in Korea, working closely with Blizzard to deliver localized content and expand the in-game rewards ecosystem. Operations are planned to begin later this year. As the publisher, Nexon will manage local live service and business operations, tailoring the game to Korean players. The collaboration will include hyper-localized content, with more details to be announced. The partnership is significant for Korean players, who will now have a localized version of the game, including a Korean hero, D.Va.

stories.headline: Steam on Linux Surpasses 5% Market Share in the Latest Survey Update
stories.summary: Linux-based operating systems have reached a 5.33% market share on Steam, surpassing the 5% threshold for the first time. According to Valve's Steam Hardware & Software Survey for March 2026, this marks an all-time high for Linux, more than doubling the macOS figure of 2.35%. The significant 3.1 percentage point increase in a single month has pushed Windows down 4.28% to 92.33%. This growth is notable, as Linux has historically held a low single-digit market share. The survey tracks the operating systems and hardware configurations of active Steam users, providing insight into the platform's user base. The latest data, reported by Phoronix, indicates a substantial shift in Linux's presence on Steam, solidifying its position as a minority but genuine force in PC gaming. This milestone suggests increasing momentum for Linux on the platform.

stories.headline: Cruciate ligament injury rules out Egypt international from the World Cup
stories.summary: Egyptian international Islam Issa has suffered a torn cruciate ligament in his knee, ruling him out of the 2026 World Cup. The injury was announced by the Egyptian Football Association, dealing a significant blow to the national team. Issa had made an impressive start with the Pharaohs before the injury. The World Cup is set to take place in less than three months, and Issa's absence will be felt. The injury is a concern for clubs with players on international duty, as Barcelona has already suffered from injuries sustained by their players while representing their countries. Issa's injury is the latest setback, and his absence from the World Cup will be a significant loss for Egypt's national team. The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has also made announcements regarding the World Cup.

stories.headline: Bastoni's red card sparks Barcelona exit fears in Italy
stories.summary: Alessandro Bastoni's red card in Italy's World Cup qualifier against Bosnia has sparked concerns about his future with the national team. The defender received a straight red card in the 41st minute, forcing Italy to play with ten men for the remainder of the game, including extra time. Italy ultimately lost, resulting in their elimination from the tournament. Italian media outlets, including TuttoSport and La Gazzetta dello Sport, strongly criticized Bastoni's actions, calling it a "disaster" and a "tackle deserving of a red card." The incident has raised questions about Bastoni's role and status within the national team, with some speculating about his potential exit. Meanwhile, FC Barcelona remains confident in Bastoni, despite the fallout from his red card and the ongoing impact of Raphinha's injury.

stories.headline: Ibrahima Konate's Liverpool wage demands revealed as contract rebel pushes for bumper pay rise amid Real Madrid transfer links
stories.summary: Ibrahima Konate's contract negotiations with Liverpool have hit a stumbling block due to his wage demands. The French defender is seeking a significant pay rise, reportedly wanting his weekly salary to more than double from £70,000 to £200,000. This would put him among Liverpool's top earners, alongside players like Mohamed Salah and Virgil van Dijk. Konate's agents are negotiating a new contract, and the player is free to speak to European clubs about a potential summer move. Despite links to Real Madrid, the Spanish club withdrew their interest before Christmas and have shown no signs of rekindling it. Liverpool has not received any bids for Konate, but the player's limited options may be a factor in his contract demands. The negotiations come after Konate faced criticism for a slow start to the season.

stories.headline: Bengals agree to one-year deal with S Kyle Dugger
stories.summary: The Cincinnati Bengals have agreed to a one-year deal with safety Kyle Dugger. Dugger, 30, will join the Bengals after spending the past four seasons with the New England Patriots and Pittsburgh Steelers. The agreement was reported by ESPN's Adam Schefter and confirmed by Dugger's agent, Drew Rosenhaus. This move adds to the Bengals' secondary, strengthening their defense. Dugger's experience in the AFC North, having played for the Steelers, will be an asset to the team. The one-year contract suggests a short-term commitment, allowing the Bengals to assess Dugger's fit with the team. The signing is the latest development in the Bengals' offseason efforts to bolster their roster.

stories.headline: Track 2 channels active between India and Pakistan even as official relations remain in freeze After the deadly 2020 Galwan Valley clash, India and China froze much official contact. But Track 2 exchanges helped keep lines open. High-level signals of thaw
stories.summary: India and Pakistan have maintained Track 2 channels of communication despite a freeze in official relations. Similarly, after the 2020 Galwan Valley clash, India and China froze official contact, but Track 2 exchanges helped keep lines open. The Galwan Valley clash occurred on June 15, 2020, near the Line of Actual Control (LAC) in eastern Ladakh, straining relations between the two countries. However, after years of negotiations, India and China reached an agreement on October 21 regarding disengagement in Demchok and Depsang areas and patrolling. Intelligence channels have also remained active, with undisclosed contacts. These efforts indicate a coordinated attempt to maintain communication through multiple channels, even when direct engagement is constrained. The high-level signals suggest a potential thaw in relations between the two countries.

stories.headline: Holding SIR judicial officers hostage in West Bengal a ‘calculated attempt’ to disrupt elections: SC Supreme Court condemns gherao of judicial officers in West Bengal, calling it a brazen challenge to judicial authority and political polarization.
stories.summary: The Supreme Court condemned the gherao of seven judicial officers in West Bengal's Malda district, calling it a brazen challenge to judicial authority and political polarization. The officers, engaged in the Summary Revision of Electoral Rolls (SIR) exercise, were held hostage for several hours by protesters over the deletion of names from electoral rolls. The protesters, who initially sought a meeting with the officers, gheraoed the Kaliachak 2 Block Development Office around 4 pm. A large contingent of security forces rescued the officers, including three women, after midnight. The Supreme Court termed the incident deplorable and sought responses from the state home secretary, DGP, and other officials for their inaction, directing a probe and deployment of central forces. The court flagged contempt and viewed the incident as a calculated attempt to disrupt elections.

stories.headline: India news: Defense exports surge to over $4 billion India's defense exports surged to a record more than $4.1 billion (€3.56 billion) in the fiscal year to March 2026.
stories.summary: India's defense exports have reached a record high of over $4.1 billion in the fiscal year to March 2026. The defense ministry reported exports of 38,424 billion rupees, driven by growing global demand for Indian-made military equipment. This significant increase indicates a surge in the country's defense industry, with Indian products gaining traction worldwide. The export figure surpasses previous records, showcasing India's emerging role as a major player in the global defense market. The growth in defense exports is a notable development for India's economy, highlighting the country's capabilities in manufacturing and exporting military equipment to meet international demand. The defense ministry's announcement underscores the government's efforts to promote India's defense industry and increase its global presence.

stories.headline: India’s new digital rules tighten the noose on freedom of speech Journalists, news outlets, satirists and cartoonists are among the spate of citizens targeted by India’s increasingly strict laws on digital censorship. Critics
stories.summary: India's government has introduced new digital rules, tightening restrictions on freedom of speech. Journalists, news outlets, satirists, and cartoonists have been targeted by the increasingly strict laws on digital censorship. Recently, the Twitter accounts of two Delhi-based journalists, Kanchan Gupta and Shiv Aroor, were blocked, and a newspaper was banned in Srinagar for containing "material that tends to incite acts of violence." The Indian government has also introduced amendments to existing laws, which critics say could further restrict freedom of expression. The move has been decried by journalists as a clampdown on democracy, particularly in Kashmir, where there has been unrest following the killing of a terrorist group commander. The government claims to respect the right to free speech, but its actions have sparked widespread criticism.

stories.headline: ‘India is 100% import‑dependent for Helium, making many industries acutely vulnerable to global disruptions’
stories.summary: India is entirely dependent on imports for Helium, making its industries vulnerable to global disruptions. The shortage of Helium has affected hospitals, forcing them to ration refills, delay installations, and prioritize emergency scans. The semiconductor and electronics industry also relies on Helium for various processes. The space industry uses Helium to cool satellite instruments and clean rocket engines. A recent disruption in Qatar, which supplies roughly one-third of global Helium production, has exacerbated the shortage. The country's energy infrastructure damage has halted some exports, affecting the global supply chain. This shortage has significant implications for India's industries, including healthcare and technology, and highlights the country's vulnerability to geopolitical conflicts and supply chain disruptions. Industry experts, however, believe that fears of MRI service disruptions in India due to Helium shortages may be overstated.

stories.headline: The Swiss franc hasn’t been playing its traditional haven role of late, even as the war in Iran keeps geopolitics fraught. Options trading suggests the anomaly
stories.summary: The Swiss franc has deviated from its traditional role as a safe-haven currency amidst the ongoing conflict in Iran. Despite heightened geopolitical tensions, the franc has not consistently behaved like a haven currency since the conflict broke out. However, options trading suggests that this anomaly may not last, with traders positioning for the Swiss National Bank to take action. The franc's recent pullback is seen in the context of its prior strength, and traders are preparing for a potential reversal. The conflict in Iran has triggered a significant energy shock, driving up fuel, food, and transportation costs, and slowing global growth. As a result, investors are closely watching the Swiss franc, anticipating a potential return to its traditional haven role. Options trading activity indicates that traders expect the franc to regain its safe-haven status.

stories.headline: How the geopolitical landscape and war in Iran are making sports fandom even more expensive
stories.summary: The escalating conflict in Iran is driving up the cost of sports fandom due to a combination of economic factors, including inflation and geopolitical instability. The Middle East's growing influence on global sports, with countries like Saudi Arabia and Qatar hosting major events and providing financial backing, is being disrupted by the US-Iran tensions. The region's ambitions to become a global sporting powerhouse are being cast into doubt, with event cancellations and disruptions affecting athletes like PV Sindhu. The war is exposing the fragility of the sports sector, leading to increased costs and uncertainty for fans. As a result, the cost of sports events and related activities is rising, making it more expensive for fans to engage with their favorite sports and teams. This trend is expected to continue as the geopolitical landscape remains volatile.

stories.headline: Markets are increasingly shaped by policy and geopolitics. Public Affairs is now strategic. Organisations need agility, credibility and foresight to manage risk
stories.summary: Markets are increasingly influenced by policy and geopolitics, making public affairs a strategic necessity for organisations. To manage risk and capture new opportunities, companies require agility, credibility, and foresight. The current geopolitical landscape, particularly the Iran war updates, is contributing to uncertainty, with messaging aimed at calming markets having diminishing credibility. Trump's desire for flexibility on the war limits his ability to offer clarity, further eroding public confidence. Geopolitics and public affairs are intertwined, with globalization's latest iteration potentially exacerbating geopolitical turmoil rather than alleviating it. Low-income countries need access to global markets to develop their manufacturing sectors, highlighting the complex interplay between business, trade, politics, and democracy in a shifting global order.

stories.headline: Sensex Crashes 1,400 Points, Nifty Near 22,250: Trump-Iran Tensions Trigger Market Selloff
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant downturn, with the Sensex plummeting by almost 1,400 points and the Nifty falling below 22,250. The market selloff was triggered by rising tensions between the US and Iran, sparked by President Trump's threat of a strike. Additionally, rising crude oil prices and selling by foreign institutional investors (FIIs) contributed to the decline. The BSE Sensex was down to 72,665.81, while the NSE Nifty slipped to 22,531.60. The surge in oil prices and weakness in the rupee have heightened inflation risks, further impacting the market. The downturn is a result of global and domestic factors, including geopolitical tensions and economic concerns. The market decline is being closely watched by investors and analysts, who are assessing the potential long-term impact on the Indian economy.

stories.headline: Stock Market Today: Sensex, Nifty50 decline 2% on rising tensions in West Asia
stories.summary: Equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty declined 2% in early trade on Thursday amid rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia. The tensions follow US President Donald Trump's warning of intensified military actions against Iran, driving crude oil prices higher. The BSE Sensex plunged 1,557 points, or 2.13%, to 71,576.35, while the Nifty 50 dropped 495 points, or 2.19%, to 22,183.60. The decline in domestic equity markets is heavily influenced by sentiment, with investors reacting to the escalating tensions in the region. The increase in crude oil prices is also a contributing factor to the decline in the markets. The uncertainty in West Asia has led to a cautious approach among investors, with some experts advising to deploy capital in a calibrated manner. Attractive sectors are being highlighted by analysts despite the prevailing uncertainty.

stories.headline: Sensex rebounds 1,800 points from day's low- Key highlights from Indian stock market today
stories.summary: The Sensex closed 185 points higher at 73,319.55, rebounding 1,800 points from its day's low of 71,545.81. The 30-share pack initially plunged 1,588.51 points, or 2.17%, but later erased all losses to end in the green. The index had vaulted 1,774 points from its low to end with modest gains. In afternoon trade, the Sensex was at 78,063.99, up 497 points, with the Nifty hovering near the 24,222 mark. The market's rebound came despite initial losses, with the government signaling a 'very important' bill soon in the Rajya Sabha. The Sensex's recovery was significant, gaining 1,800 points from its intraday low to close higher. The Indian stock market's key highlights included a volatile trading session with significant intraday swings.

stories.headline: Stock Market Crash Update: Sensex Falls 1,400 Points, Trump’s War Warning & Global Risks Trigger Sharp Sell-Off
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex plummeted 1,411 points to 71,716.82, a 1.92% decline, while the NSE Nifty fell 445 points to 22,210.67, a 1.98% drop. This sharp sell-off was triggered by rising geopolitical tensions, particularly US President Trump's war warning, and uncertainty in global markets. All 16 major sectors declined, with heavyweight financials and banks losing 1.6% each. The broader small-caps and mid-caps fell 1.5% and 1.2%, respectively. The sudden fall came after a strong rally the previous day, where the Sensex had gained over 1,100 points and the Nifty had risen 348 points. The decline reflects investor concerns over escalating Iran war tensions and rising crude oil prices, with the Nifty 50 and Sensex falling 16% and 17% from their lifetime highs.

stories.headline: Iran war will debilitate petrochemicals for the rest of 2026 Chemical production will decline in Asia as long as the conflict persists, experts say
stories.summary: The ongoing Iran war is expected to debilitate the petrochemical industry for the rest of 2026, with chemical production declining in Asia as long as the conflict persists. Significant energy infrastructure has already been destroyed, and rebuilding efforts will take years. The war's impact will also be felt in 2027 due to crop losses resulting from a lack of fertilizer. Iranian officials have selected Mojtaba Khamenei as the successor to the late supreme leader, but US President Trump has signaled he does not support the choice. Trump claims the war could end in "two to three weeks" without Iran needing to make a deal, while Iran's Foreign Minister confirms messages have been exchanged with the US, but denies negotiations. The conflict has led to a shift in the global energy trade balance and a surge in oil prices.

stories.headline: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.
stories.summary: Microsoft's research lab, Microsoft AI, has released three foundational AI models that can generate text, voice, and images. The models, developed by the company's MAI division, include a voice-to-text transcription model, an audio generation model, and an image synthesis model. One of the models, called Voicebox, is a generative AI model for speech that can generalize across tasks with state-of-the-art performance. The release of these models challenges rivals such as OpenAI and Google, and is seen as a way for Microsoft to reduce its reliance on external AI systems. The models are available through Microsoft's Foundry platform, and are expected to provide lower-cost alternatives to existing AI systems. The launch comes six months after the formation of the MAI group, and demonstrates Microsoft's commitment to developing its own AI capabilities.

stories.headline: A Look At Ciena (CIEN) Valuation After Vodafone Idea AI Optical Network Win Vodafone Idea’s decision to modernize its transport network with Ciena (CIEN) WaveLogic 6 Extreme coherent optical technology, including 1.6 Tb/s data center li
stories.summary: Ciena has won a contract with Vodafone Idea to modernize its transport network in India using WaveLogic 6 Extreme coherent optical technology. This deal highlights Ciena's growth potential in high-capacity optical transport, driven by rising mobile data, cloud connectivity, and video traffic. The company's share price has surged 77.6% in the past 90 days and 68.8% year to date, with a 1-year total shareholder return of over 5x. Ciena expects revenues of approximately $2.6 billion in the first quarter of 2026, with double-digit revenue and net income growth. The company's technology is being used to support growing bandwidth demands and pursue new business opportunities with hyperscalers, neoscalers, and enterprise customers, positioning it for further growth in the AI infrastructure market. The deal raises questions about whether Ciena's valuation already reflects its growth potential.

stories.headline: Ducommun Stock: Missile Growth Offsets Boeing Destocking Headwinds Ducommun (DCO) stock stays a Buy with a $159.94 target and 12.7% annualized upside through 2028—defense-driven growth, margin gains, and valuation upside.
stories.summary: Ducommun Incorporated (DCO) stock is expected to experience 12.7% annualized upside through 2028, driven by defense-driven growth, margin gains, and valuation upside. The company's missile growth is offsetting headwinds from Boeing destocking. Despite this, the stock has faced challenges, including a secondary offering priced below its previous closing price, which led to a decline in stock value. Currently, Ducommun stock has a target price of $159.94 and is considered a Buy. The company's financial performance and stock activity can be monitored through real-time stock prices and quotes, with updates on news and vital information available for investors. Ducommun's stock performance is closely watched, with some analysts providing reasons to avoid the stock, while others recommend it as a buying opportunity.

stories.headline: Microsoft aims to create large cutting-edge AI models by 2027
stories.summary: Microsoft aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by 2027, as part of a push to build in-house alternatives to powerful AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic. The company plans to integrate and diversify AI models, enhancing products like Microsoft 365 Copilot with both internal and third-party technology. With $50 billion earmarked for global AI expansion, Microsoft seeks to innovate and cut costs for enterprise users. The company's AI lead has stated that the core objective is to create the most advanced models in the industry, achieving world-leading capabilities in text, image, and audio processing with proprietary models by 2027. This strategic shift aims to reduce reliance on OpenAI and establish Microsoft as a leader in the AI industry. Microsoft is investing heavily in AI research to build a cutting-edge system that can rival OpenAI and Anthropic.

stories.headline: OpenAI’s Next AI Model ‘Spud’ Could Be A Major Leap Toward AGI, Says Greg Brockman
stories.summary: OpenAI President Greg Brockman has teased the company's next AI model, codenamed "Spud." According to Brockman, Spud will mark a significant leap for OpenAI and may bring the company closer to achieving Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Spud is not a minor update, but an entirely new base model that will support the development of all future models. Brockman described it as being worth two years of research and a major step toward AGI. The model is expected to combine the strengths of general-purpose AI with advanced reasoning capabilities, potentially sitting at the top of OpenAI's expanding model lineup. Brockman's comments suggest that Spud could be a major breakthrough in AI development, with significant implications for the field. The exact details and capabilities of Spud have not been fully disclosed, but it is expected to be a significant advancement.

stories.headline: As Disney’s deal with OpenAI falls apart, what next for AI in Hollywood?
stories.summary: Disney has cancelled its deal with OpenAI, a $1 billion investment agreed upon in December. The deal allowed OpenAI's video generator, Sora, to use Disney characters. However, OpenAI shut down Sora, and the investment never went through. The cancellation is a result of OpenAI's decision to exit the video generation business. Disney's new CEO, Josh D'Amaro, has put the relationship with OpenAI on hold. The move raises questions about the future of AI in Hollywood, as OpenAI faces pressure to wisely spend the billions invested in it. The collapse of the deal indicates a shift in Disney's strategy under new leadership, and the entertainment industry is watching to see how AI technology will be utilized in the future. OpenAI's exit from video generation may impact its partnerships with other studios.

stories.headline: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year, part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most power
stories.summary: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year as part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most powerful AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic. The company's goal is to deliver state-of-the-art AI models, with Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of Microsoft AI, emphasizing the need to "deliver the absolute frontier." This strategic pivot has profound implications across various sectors, underscoring the transformative potential and challenges of AI. Microsoft's decision to develop its own in-house AI models is a key part of its growth strategy, with CEO Satya Nadella noting that this generation of AI is "radically changing every layer of the tech stack." The company is embracing the AI platform shift, positioning itself for future growth and innovation in the rapidly evolving tech landscape. Development of these models is targeted for completion by 2027.
